title: dryopteris wollastonii
intro: Dryopteris wollastonii, also known as Wollaston's Male Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating parasitic worm infestations, such as tapeworms and hookworms, due to its anthelmintic properties. The health benefits of Wollaston's Male Fern include its ability to expel parasites from the body, reduce inflammation, and support the digestive system. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and processing the fronds, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or capsules for consumption.
